text: Code reviews are an essential part of software development. In a code review, one or more team members carefully examine a colleague's code before it is added to the main project. This process is highly valuable for several reasons. First, it helps catch bugs and logic errors early, saving time and money that would be spent fixing them later. Second, code reviews promote knowledge sharing. Developers learn new techniques, best practices, and different parts of the codebase. Finally, regular reviews improve overall team quality by encouraging clear, consistent coding standards. In short, code reviews build better software and stronger teams.
